title: Pallipuram Fort
tagline: Pallipuram Fort — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Pallippuram Fort or (Paleport Castelo em Cima) is a fort in Pallippuram, Vyppin, Ernakulam district of Kerala, south India.
overview.description: Pallippuram Fort or (Paleport Castelo em Cima) is a fort in Pallippuram, Vyppin, Ernakulam district of Kerala, south India. It was built by Portuguese sailors on 27 September, 1503 using just timber wood, and later renovated in 1505 by replacing timber structure with stone. It is the oldest existing European fort in India. The Dutch captured the fort in 1663 and sold it to the Kingdom of Travancore in 1789.

topPlaces.description: The Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram, Ernakulam, Kerala, is a minor basilica. The basilica decree was issued on 27 August 2012.

topPlaces.name: Pallippuram, Ernakulam
topPlaces.description: Pallippuram (also known in English as Palliport or in Portuguese as Paliporte) is a village on Vypeen island, in Kochi, Kerala, south India. The village is located approximately 25 km from Ernakulam and 20 km from Cochin International Airport.

topPlaces.name: Munambam
topPlaces.description: Munambam is a suburb of Kochi, India at the north end of Vypeen Island, surrounded by the Arabian Sea on the west, Periyar river on the east, and a mouth of the sea on the north. The main occupation of its inhabitants is fishing and related activities.

topPlaces.name: Vavakkad
topPlaces.description: Vavakkad is a village situated along the northern border of Ernakulam District, Paravur taluk, Kerala, South India.

topPlaces.name: Periyar River
topPlaces.description: The Periyar (Malayalam: [peɾijaːr], meaning: big river) is the longest river and has the largest discharge potential in the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the few perennial rivers in the region and provides drinking water for several major towns.

topPlaces.name: Sathar Island
topPlaces.description: Sathar Island (10°11′26.34″N 76°11′28.88″E) is an island located in a distributary of the Periyar River in the Ernakulam district of the Indian state of Kerala. The land strip is about 2 km in length and 156 acres in area.

topPlaces.name: Moothakunnam
topPlaces.description: Moothakunnam is a small village in the Paravur Taluk, Ernakulam district of Kerala, South India.

topPlaces.name: Kuriapilly
topPlaces.description: Kuriappilly now known as Kuriappilly, Kuriappilly South, labor Junction, Shappupadi (Toddy parlor ferry, Kappelapadi. Kuriapilly was a border of Travancore kingdom in the British India, the name Kuriapilly is old up to 500 years, even in the letters of Tippu Sultan we can see the name Kuriapilly.

items.activity: Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram
items.note: The Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram, Ernakulam, Kerala, is a minor basilica. The basilica decree was issued on 27 August 2012.

Pallippuram (also known in English as Palliport or in Portuguese as Paliporte) is a village on Vypeen island, in Kochi, Kerala, south India. The village is located approximately 25 km from Ernakulam and 20 km from Cochin International Airport.

Munambam is a suburb of Kochi, India at the north end of Vypeen Island, surrounded by the Arabian Sea on the west, Periyar river on the east, and a mouth of the sea on the north. The main occupation of its inhabitants is fishing and related activities.
Vavakkad is a village situated along the northern border of Ernakulam District, Paravur taluk, Kerala, South India.

The Periyar (Malayalam: [peɾijaːr], meaning: big river) is the longest river and has the largest discharge potential in the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the few perennial rivers in the region and provides drinking water for several major towns.

Sathar Island (10°11′26.34″N 76°11′28.88″E) is an island located in a distributary of the Periyar River in the Ernakulam district of the Indian state of Kerala. The land strip is about 2 km in length and 156 acres in area.
Moothakunnam is a small village in the Paravur Taluk, Ernakulam district of Kerala, South India.

Kuriappilly now known as Kuriappilly, Kuriappilly South, labor Junction, Shappupadi (Toddy parlor ferry, Kappelapadi. Kuriapilly was a border of Travancore kingdom in the British India, the name Kuriapilly is old up to 500 years, even in the letters of Tippu Sultan we can see the name Kuriapilly.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pallipuram Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pallipuram Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pallipuram Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pallipuram Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pallipuram Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pallipuram Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Pallipuram Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pallipuram Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram, Pallippuram, Ernakulam, Munambam, Vavakkad, Periyar River.
